channel.title: math.MG updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.MG updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Gromov-Hausdorff distance and Jung constant of finite-dimensional normed spaces
item.description: arXiv:2607.18447v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For a finite-dimensional normed space $V$ and a subset $X$ with finite Hausdorff distance from $V$, we prove that the Gromov--Hausdorff distance between $X$ and $V$ is at least the Hausdorff distance between $X$ and $V$, divided by twice the relative Jung constant of $V$. If $V$ furthermore satisfies a certain intersection property, we show a stronger result where the relative Jung constant can be replaced with its absolute version. Key words: Normed spaces, Jung constant, Hausdorff distance, Gromov--Hausdorff distance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Adams, Semeon A. Bogatyi, Florian Frick, Daniil A. Ilyukhin, Alexander O. Ivanov, Ivan N. Mikhailov, Alexey A. Tuzhilin, Anton A. Vikhrov

item.title: Regular ultrametric skeletons
item.description: arXiv:2607.18525v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The theorem of Mendel and Naor (2013) associates to every compact metric probability space a large ultrametric subset carrying a probability measure controlled from above on balls by the original measure. Building on Bartal's Ramsey decompositions (2021) and the author's previous proofs of the ultrametric skeleton theorem for doubling spaces (2022, 2023), we prove a regular version for arbitrary compact metric spaces with a linear dilation of the control balls.
item.dc:creator: Manor Mendel

item.title: All-angles bisection of polygon area
item.description: arXiv:2607.18967v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Given a simple polygon we aim to find the equation of the straight line which bisects the area of the polygon in a given direction. Additionally, we would like to vary this direction with minimal additional calculation. We provide a solution in the case where the polygon is `bisection-convex', meaning that any straight line bisecting the area of the polygon contains exactly two points on the boundary of the polygon.
item.dc:creator: Robin Whitty

item.title: A reverse entropy power inequality for i.i.d. log-concave random variables
item.description: arXiv:2510.09206v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We show that $h_\infty(X+Y)\leq h_\infty(Z+W)$, where $X, Y$ are independent log-concave random variables, and $Z, W$ are exponential random variables having the same respective $\infty$-R\'enyi entropies. Analogs for integer-valued monotone log-concave random variables are also obtained. Our main tools are decreasing rearrangement, majorization, and the change of measure.
item.dc:creator: Zhen Fu, Jiange Li

item.title: Quadratic metric comparisons
item.description: arXiv:2512.03353v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We study the effects on length spaces imposed by quadratic inequalities on the six distances between the points in every quadruple.
item.dc:creator: Nina Lebedeva, Anton Petrunin, Vladimir Zolotov

item.title: Coarse higher medians, symmetric spaces, and convex projective geometry
item.description: arXiv:2607.05036v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We introduce a notion of coarse $r$-median spaces that is a higher-rank analog of Bowditch's coarse median spaces. Our notion is stable under quasi-isometries and recovers Bowditch's coarse medians when $r$ equals 1. We prove that several families of higher rank-symmetric spaces admit coarse $r$-medians with $r$ being the rank of the symmetric space. In particular, our list includes plenty of examples that are known to not admit Bowditch's coarse medians. Our main tools come from convex projective geometry, and we prove the existence of coarse higher medians on all divisible as well as quasi-homogeneous properly convex domains.
item.dc:creator: Mitul Islam, Grazia Rago
